📝 Speech To Text: hello doctor I'm facing acne on my face what can I do
🩺 Doctor Response:
The image shows a person's face with acne, specifically on the forehead and nose. I see multiple small, raised red bumps, likely comedones and papules.

Medically, this appears to be a case of mild to moderate acne vulgaris. 

Differential diagnoses include folliculitis, rosacea, and keratosis pilaris, but based on the image, acne vulgaris is most likely.

Remedies include topical retinoids to prevent clogged pores, benzoyl peroxide to reduce bacteria, and gentle cleansing. For more severe cases, oral antibiotics may be necessary. A dermatologist can provide personalized guidance and prescribe treatments.
